text: I stand before a glass wall, peering into a room awash with color, lit by glowing letters that proclaim "R+D." The ambiance hums with anticipation, as if ideas themselves are about to materialize. I feel a quiet thrill at the promise of innovation—curious, on the verge of discovery, yet keenly aware of the boundaries between inside and out. The philosophical possibilities are palpable, inviting me inward to reflect on the nature of research and development, and my place in this evolving landscape.
